title: Podman pod stats -- fix GO template output
body:
Go templates were not being processed or printed correctly for podman
pod stats.  Added the ability to do templates as well as honor the
table identifier.

Fixes #2258

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I'm a bit concerned that we're adding tabwriter stuff back into the main code path, which could have the same perf implications that `podman ps` had that we needed a rewrite for.

On the other hand, I doubt we see as many pods as we did containers, so let's cross that bridge when we come to it.

/lgtm

> I'm a bit concerned that we're adding tabwriter stuff back into the main code path, which could have the same perf implications that `podman ps` had that we needed a rewrite for.
> 
> On the other hand, I doubt we see as many pods as we did containers, so let's cross that bridge when we come to it.
> 
> /lgtm

@mheon it wasnt tabwriter that had the perf implications, it was the use of the go templating for regular output and the multiple serializations needed for that.
